hawkish_phrases: ongoing tightening of monetary policy over the forecast period...would be appropriate
excess demand begins to build...rises above 1% of potential output by the end of the forecast period
CPI inflation picks up to above the 2% target in two years' time and is still rising
unit labour cost growth has strengthened to rates that are above historical averages
domestic inflationary pressures are expected to firm
dovish_phrases: path for Bank Rate that rises to around 1% by the end of the forecast period, lower than in the February Report
CPI inflation was 1.9% in March and is expected to be slightly further below the MPC's 2% target
the current stance of monetary policy is appropriate
the underlying pace of GDP growth appears...marginally below potential
there is currently a small margin of excess supply in the economy
summary: The MPC voted unanimously to hold Bank Rate at 0.75%, with guidance for ongoing gradual tightening over the forecast period but a lower rate path than February, reflecting building excess demand and above-target inflation projections despite current slack.
